channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President says China undermining peace, warns against annexation
item.description: President Lai Ching-te stated Taiwan would never accept annexation by China, saying the island's future cannot be decided by external forces. The comments drew an angry reaction from Beijing. — Reuters

item.title: 🛢️ Chinese supertankers exit Hormuz as Trump and Vance signal Iran deal progress
item.description: Two Chinese supertankers carrying 4 million barrels of crude exited the Strait of Hormuz after waiting two months, as US officials expressed optimism about negotiations with Tehran. — Al Jazeera

item.title: ⚔️ Russia displays nuclear warhead transfers in major military exercise
item.description: Russia released footage of troops delivering nuclear warheads to mobile Iskander-M launch systems and moving them undetected to launch sites as part of a major military exercise. — Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Four US LNG vessels sailing to China following Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Four liquefied natural gas vessels are en route to China from the US, marking the first direct LNG cargoes during Trump's second term amid ongoing trade negotiations. — Reuters

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon kill 19, including children and women
item.description: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 19 people, including four women and three children, amid ongoing Israel-Hezbollah conflict despite U.S.-brokered ceasefire extensions. — The Hindu

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ US Senate Advances Resolution to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers
item.description: The US Senate moved forward with a procedural vote to curb presidential authority on Iran military actions, though the resolution faces significant hurdles before becoming law. — Reuters

item.title: ☢️🇷🇺 Russia Conducts Major Nuclear Drills Amid Ongoing Tensions
item.description: Russia began three days of nuclear drills including ballistic and cruise missile launches, the defense ministry announced, as Moscow continues its confrontation with the West over Ukraine. — Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 NATO Commander: US Troop Cuts from Europe to Take Years
item.description: NATO's top commander said US troop withdrawals from Europe will stretch over years to give allies time to develop replacement capabilities, maintaining alliance defense posture. — Reuters

item.title: ⚠️🇱🇻 Russia Warns Latvia Against Ukraine Drone Launches
item.description: Russia's UN ambassador claimed Ukraine plans to launch military drones from Latvia and other Baltic states, warning that NATO membership would not protect them from retaliation. — Reuters

item.title: 💰🌐 G7 Finance Ministers Address Trade Imbalances, China Concerns
item.description: G7 finance ministers agreed on the need to tackle trade imbalances in a fragmented global economy, with US Treasury Secretary Bessent calling for protections against cheap Chinese imports. — Reuters

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺 Xi Hosts Putin in Beijing, Hails Ties as "Calm Amid Chaos"
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping welcomed Vladimir Putin for a state visit just days after hosting Trump, calling Russia-China relations a force of stability. Xi urged an "early end" to the Iran war and criticized "unilateral hegemonic currents" in a veiled jab at the US. — CNN

item.title: 🇺🇦 UN: Over 15,800 Killed in Russia-Ukraine War Since 2022
item.description: The UN reports at least 15,850 people including 791 children have been killed since Russia's full-scale invasion. The actual figures are likely significantly higher as peace talks remain stalled. — Al Jazeera

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇸 EU Strikes Deal on US Trade Pact Ahead of Trump Tariff Deadline
item.description: The European Parliament and Council reached a late-night provisional agreement to remove import duties on US goods, keeping the bloc on track to meet the July 4 deadline and avoid higher US tariffs on European goods. — Deutsche Welle

item.title: 🇬🇧⛽ UK quietly sanctions Russian oil via waivers as energy costs surge
item.description: Britain issued licenses allowing imports of diesel and jet fuel made from Russian crude processed in third countries, and maritime transport of Russian LNG. The move follows similar US waivers as fuel prices spike during the Iran conflict. — Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇺🇦💥 Ukraine strikes Russian oil refinery and chemical plant for second time in a week
item.description: Ukrainian drones hit the Lukoil-Nizhegorodnefteorgsintez refinery in Nizhny Novgorod Oblast and the Nevinnomyssk Azot chemical plant in Stavropol Krai overnight. Both facilities had been struck earlier in the week. — Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇨🇿🇪🇺 Czechia proposes restrictions on Ukrainian refugee benefits
item.description: The Czech Interior Ministry proposed legal changes requiring refugees not to leave Schengen for more than 30 days, be economically active to receive benefits, and register vehicles. EU discussions on long-term protections continue amid uncertainty. — Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Vance says US not planning to send Iranian uranium to Russia
item.description: VP Vance dismissed reports that the US would allow Russia to take possession of Iran enriched uranium as part of a peace deal, saying it was not the current plan. Tehran reportedly offered to transfer 400kg of enriched uranium to Russia in leaked settlement details. — Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇷🇺🇨🇳 Putin declares Russia-China ties at unprecedented level, invites Xi to Moscow
item.description: President Putin told Xi Jinping that bilateral relations have reached an unprecedented level during Beijing talks, extending an invitation for Xi to visit Russia next year. — Reuters

item.title: 🇷🇺💰 Russia considers joint projects with US and China, says sovereign fund chief
item.description: Kirill Dmitriev, head of Russia sovereign wealth fund RDIF, indicated Moscow is exploring potential joint economic initiatives with both Washington and Beijing. — Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🏦 Fed Chair nominee Warsh signals shift on independence, unsettling global central bankers
item.description: Kevin Warsh suggestion that Fed independence may not fully extend to crisis-fighting abroad has raised concerns among central banking peers worldwide. — Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Treasury Secretary Bessent calls for stronger action against Iran financing networks
item.description: Scott Bessent urged allies to more forcefully disrupt Iran financial channels and announced a review to scrub outdated sanctions designations for better enforcement. — Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US not rushing to extend China tariff truce, Bessent says
item.description: Treasury Secretary indicated the administration has time to negotiate extensions before the November expiration, signaling no urgency on the critical minerals deal. — Reuters

item.title: ⚔️ Trump Was "An Hour Away" From Ordering Iran Strike Before Postponing
item.description: President Trump said he had made the decision to attack Iran before Gulf allies requested a delay. He gave Iran "two or three days" to reach a deal, keeping military options ready. — CNBC

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Calls Off Iran Attack Amid "Serious Negotiations"
item.description: The president postponed planned strikes on Iran at the request of Gulf Arab states, claiming serious negotiations are underway. Iran shows no signs of budging on key demands despite threats. — CBS News

item.title: 🇯🇵 Swedish defense giant Saab expands role in Japan defense buildup
item.description: Swedish defense contractor Saab is positioning for a larger weapons supplier role in Japan, building on growing economic ties as Japan accelerates its military modernization. — Nikkei Asia
